steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Find the area of Circle N
steps.content: Circle N has radius 8 mm. Use the area formula with $r = 8$: $$A_N = \pi(8)^2 = 64\pi \text{ mm}^2$$

steps.title: Add both areas to find the total
steps.content: Circle M's area is $81\pi$ mm² (given). Adding Circle N and Circle M: $$64\pi + 81\pi = 145\pi \text{ mm}^2$$ That confirms A is correct.
